source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Extra study sessions were offered to students after the midterm to help improve their understanding of statistics. Student scores on the midterm and the final exam were recorded. The following scatterplot shows final test scores against the midterm test scores. Which of the following statements correctly interprets the scatterplot?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: Students who scored below 55 on the midterm showed considerable improvement on the final exam; those who scored between 55 and 80 on the midterm showed minimal improvement on the final exam; and those who scored above 80 on the midterm showed almost no improvement on the final exam.

choice_map.A: All students have shown significant improvement in the final exam scores as a result of the extra study sessions.
choice_map.B: The extra study sessions were of no help. Each student’s final exam score was about the same as his or her score on the midterm.
choice_map.C: The extra study sessions further confused students. All student scores decreased from midterm to final exam.
choice_map.D: Students who scored below 55 on the midterm showed considerable improvement on the final exam; those who scored between 55 and 80 on the midterm showed minimal improvement on the final exam; and those who scored above 80 on the midterm showed almost no improvement on the final exam.
choice_map.E: Students who scored below 55 on the midterm showed minimal improvement on the final exam; those who scored between 55 and 80 on the midterm showed moderate improvement on the final exam; and those who scored above 80 on the midterm showed considerable improvement on the final exam.
